The breakfast and dinner buffets are amazing spreads of international cuisine including Thai, Chinese, Sri Lankan, Italian, etc.

Name: Cinnamon Citadel Kandy
User_Review:
Our stay at Cinnamon Citadel Kandy was nothing short of magical! From the moment we arrived, we were surrounded by the calm beauty of the river, the lush greenery, and the signature warmth of Sri Lankan hospitality. The entire property radiates tranquility — perfect for anyone looking to relax, unwind, and soak in Kandy’s charm.

But what truly made our experience stand out were the amazing staff members who went above and beyond to make us feel special.
A heartfelt thank you to Sasi, Madusanku, Misshanka, and Aruna — each of them was exceptional in their own way! Their attentiveness, smiles, and genuine care made every meal and moment memorable. They treated us not like guests, but like family — always checking in, suggesting the best local dishes, and ensuring every detail was perfect.

The food was outstanding — a beautiful mix of local flavors and international favorites, served with elegance and heart. Whether it was breakfast by the river or dinner under the stars, every meal was a delightful experience.

The rooms were spacious, clean, and offered breathtaking views of the Mahaweli River.

If you’re visiting Kandy, look no further — Cinnamon Citadel Kandy is a slice of paradise.
Thanks again to Sasi, Madusanku, Misshanka, and Aruna for making our stay unforgettable. You truly represent the best of Sri Lankan hospitality! 💛🇱🇰

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ — Highly recommended!

Name: Cinnamon Citadel Kandy
User_Review:
Excellent Stay Once Again at Cinnamon Citadel Kandy

This was my second stay at the Cinnamon Citadel Kandy, and once again, it did not disappoint. My wife and I had a wonderful, relaxing time. The hotel offers a wide variety of delicious food, and the buffet options for breakfast, lunch, and dinner were impressive. Every meal was a treat!

The staff were friendly, attentive, and always ready to help, which made our stay even more comfortable. We especially enjoyed the swimming pool – it has a beautiful view overlooking the lake, creating a peaceful and scenic atmosphere.

Overall, we had a very enjoyable and relaxing experience. We’ll definitely return again in the future!

Name: Cinnamon Citadel Kandy
User_Review: We had a great stay at the Citadel.  Rooms and service were awesome.  Had half board. Very good breakfast and dinner buffets.  We also took a one hour boat tour offered at a very reasonable price.  Adikari and Chamini were our guides and the did excellent.  Pointing out wildlife and points of interest.

Name: Cinnamon Citadel Kandy
User_Review:
We had a truly wonderful stay at Cinnamon Citadel Kandy. From the moment we arrived, the staff greeted us warmly and made us feel at home. The rooms were very clean, spacious, and well-maintained, with the added bonus of a breathtaking view of the Mahaweli River.

The food was absolutely delicious with a wide variety of choices that catered to both local and international tastes. Our child enjoyed every moment of the stay, especially the family-friendly atmosphere and the open areas around the property.

What stood out the most was the staff — always friendly, attentive, and willing to go the extra mile to make our stay comfortable. Their genuine hospitality made the experience even more memorable.

Overall, Cinnamon Citadel Kandy is the perfect place for relaxation, enjoying nature, and experiencing true Sri Lankan hospitality. We look forward to visiting again soon and highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Kandy.

Name: Grand Kandyan Hotel
User_Review:
One of best hotels I have been in Sri lanka.   The location is simply amazing with the panoramic view of Kandy city. The hotel staff were so kind, friendly and very professional. The interior is very unique and beautiful, giving a royal atmosphere. The room we were given is clean, spacious and arranged very well. The restaurant is in the 4th floor with superb view of the hill country. The food was delicious and served on time. The swimming pool is on the top floor, well maintained and there is two separate pools for children and babies. I highly recommend for enjoyable and satisfying stay at The Grand Kandiyan.
Thank you very much for your highly valued hospitality.

Name: Grand Kandyan Hotel
User_Review:
Looks great on the face of it but needs a lot of work on upkeep.

Staff was attentive and housekeeping and room service was quick. Took approx 10 mins to complete the checkin process which was smooth.

Views from the room are nice, you get a good view of the nearby hills and the buddha statue and a stupa

The guest list was made available when making the booking which clearly mentioned a kid in the room without an extra bed, still was allocated a twin bed instead of double bed.

Shower in one of the rooms was broken and wasn't fixed in the 2 days stay. Only solution provided to any problems highlighted was a room change which was going to be a hassle considering the age groups in the family.

Pro Tips -  Avoid getting a room on the entrance side of the hotel, it overlooks a school hardly 50 meters from the hotel building and can get very noisy on school mornings. Not to mention the inattentive kids in class constantly peeking through the classroom window looking at the balconies.

The AC outdoor unit is placed directly in the balcony and is very loud and throws warm air directly on your face when you are seated on  the chairs. Best is to switch off the AC before stepping into the balcony.

Name: Grand Kandyan Hotel
User_Review:
It is truly a privilege to commend Mr. Ramith Nanayakkara, Public Relations Executive at the Grand Kandyan Hotel, for his outstanding professionalism and exceptional service.

Mr. Ramith is a dynamic and approachable personality who consistently demonstrates excellence in public relations. His warm and engaging manner, combined with remarkable communication skills, ensures that every guest and client feels valued and respected. He has an admirable ability to represent the Grand Kandyan brand with elegance, creating a lasting impression of trust and hospitality.

His dedication to maintaining the hotel’s prestigious image, along with his proactive approach in handling guests’ needs, makes him a true asset to the Grand Kandyan team. Whether it’s building strong client relationships, managing events, or ensuring seamless communication, Mr. Ramith always goes above and beyond expectations.

In every interaction, he reflects the Grand Kandyan’s reputation for luxury, class, and unmatched hospitality. His professionalism, courtesy, and commitment are genuinely commendable.

⭐ Highly recommended as an exemplary professional in public relations.

Name: Grand Kandyan Hotel
User_Review: I had the pleasure of visiting Grand Kandyan last night with my girlfriend, and the location offered a truly beautiful view. I sincerely appreciate the excellent service provided by the staff. During our time at the pool bar, the service was exceptional, and I would like to extend special thanks to Bar Supervisor Thilak, who was exceptionally helpful. Despite being at the far edge of the pool area, his service was consistently attentive and personalized, which is increasingly rare among other five-star hotels. I truly commend his supervision and genuine hospitality. Additionally, I would like to acknowledge the assistance of two other staff members, Thiwan and Vishakthi, who were also very helpful. I look forward to visiting this wonderful property again and would gladly recommend it to my friends for a memorable experience.

Name: Grand Kandyan Hotel
User_Review:
Stayed for a night due to office get together in 2024.

Rooms were clean and spacious and equipped with all necessary appliances and items. Bathrooms offered some hi end experience, including a bathtub which my kid enjoyed a lot.

Service was superb, they were working closely with our company personnel and we didn't have to run around to fix issues or restock food items in our event. Food items available in buffets were really good and had range.

Location wise, you can see some nice views of the Kandy city and green mountains. Has a big swimming pool with a deep end of 6ft+.

Overall enjoyed our stay and recommending to others.

Name: Theva Residency Kandy
User_Review:
We chose Theva Residency for our honeymoon, and it couldn’t have been more perfect! 💕 We stayed in the deluxe room with a jacuzzi, and the view was simply breathtaking – the kind that makes you want to stay forever. Our 2-night stay was magical from start to finish.

The dining experience was another highlight – every meal was delicious, especially the 3-course menu that kept us excited for what’s next. Their desserts deserve a special mention too – absolutely delightful! 🍰

Above all, the staff were outstanding – warm, attentive, and truly among the best we’ve met in Kandy. 🌿✨

If you’re looking for a romantic, cozy, and unforgettable getaway in Kandy, I can’t recommend Theva Residency enough!

Name: Theva Residency Kandy
User_Review:
Positives:
Dining Experience: The culinary offerings, led by Chef Herath, were flavorful and well-presented, though portion sizes were occasionally small and prices relatively high for a 3-star property.

Housekeeping: The housekeeping staff was courteous, attentive, and responsive to guest needs.

Location: Situated in a serene, secluded area, the hotel offers a peaceful environment with a scenic road ideal for leisurely walks, despite occasional stray dog concerns noted by reception.

Views: Rooms and the lobby balcony provide picturesque views, enhancing the overall ambiance.

Areas for Improvement:
Room Quality: The rooms are compact and cleanliness standards fell short of expectations for a 3-star hotel. Cushions were uncomfortable, impacting guest comfort.

Bathroom Design: The washroom was small, with an impractical stone-laid shower design that was uncomfortable to use.

Suite Misrepresentation: The "suite" category was misleading, essentially a standard room with a jacuzzi, priced at LKR 50,000 per day (bed & breakfast), which did not justify the cost.

Dining Costs: Meals were expensive, with an additional LKR 32,000 incurred over two days beyond the bed & breakfast rate, which is steep for a 3-star establishment.

Swimming Pool: The pool appeared uninviting and lacked appeal.

Service: Some servers displayed inconsistent attitudes, occasionally coming across as unfriendly.

Accessibility: The property lacks wheelchair access, making navigation challenging for guests with mobility issues.

Parking: Limited parking spaces were available, which could inconvenience guests with vehicles.

Payment Process: The requirement to pay the full balance upon arrival, unlike standard hotel practices, felt unusual. Additionally, the advance payment was directed to a different bank account (explained as the parent company’s account), and the final payment to another, raising concerns about transparency.

Booking Confirmation: The booking process lacked efficiency. Despite a 50% advance payment made via phone, no confirmation email was provided until repeatedly requested, and the eventual receipt only confirmed the advance while requesting the remaining balance upon arrival.

Name: Theva Residency Kandy
User_Review:
Theva is a hidden gem!
My room had the most amazing view I've ever seen. The bed was large and so comfortable. I'm vegan and the restaurant made me such high quality food. The hotel is located about 10 minutes from the train station and you could easily get here with a tuktuk for about 1000LKR. The stuff was really sweet too. I would recommend staying here if you want to get out of the city for some relaxation.

Name: Theva Residency Kandy
User_Review:
Amazing place! In the mountains with an amazing view over Kandy city. Secluded from city noise, but close enough to get to the city in tuk tuk in 10 minutes. The service is world class and they are so kind and helpful - food in the restaurant is great.
Spacious, light and very clean rooms with a great balcony with the gorgeous view. Highly recommend!

Name: Theva Residency Kandy
User_Review: Very quite and nice place. Superb view point. Good hospitality service. Delicious food.

Name: OZO Hotel Kandy
User_Review: Excellent hotel in Kandy, Sri Lanka. Room is good with all conceivable amenities, including many universal wall sockets and USB ports. Breakfast and dinner very good, with multiple choices. Staff quality is excellent and they represent the human qualities of Sri Lanka, where I found that the people are one of the nicest and friendliest on earth. The hotel's location is good. Inside the guest room they have the best Sri Lankan teabags as complimentary amenity. This hotel is highly recommended.

Name: OZO Hotel Kandy
User_Review:
The service at the hotel is excellent, with attentive and friendly staff.
Rooms are clean, well-maintained, and comfortable.
Bathrooms are spotless, and all fixtures and amenities work perfectly.
The pool is nice, clean, and inviting for a relaxing swim.
Breakfast offers a good variety and is fresh and satisfying.
Dinner, however, was quite disappointing during my visit.
For Indian vegetarians, the options were limited and heavy, mostly rich gravies and oily fried items, with very little variety to balance the meal.
There was no chicken curry, and overall, meat dishes were scarce; the buffet only had cuttlefish, with no prawns or other proper meat options, leaving the dinner feeling incomplete.
Even though it’s a 4-star hotel, there’s almost no food at the Action Station, which makes it look more like a formality than an actual live-cooking experience.
One would expect freshly prepared items such as grilled meats, seafood, or even simple pasta at a 4-star property, but the section was nearly empty and added no real value to the dinner.
Not everyone enjoys Indian food in the same way, so relying too heavily on a few heavy curries doesn’t meet the expectations of a diverse group of guests.
I strongly recommend offering a wider and more balanced dinner menu, with lighter choices, more live-cooked items, and a better mix of vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes to suit different tastes.

Name: OZO Hotel Kandy
User_Review:
I really enjoyed my stay at Radisson Hotel Kandy! The place is beautiful , the rooms are super confy, and the view of Kandy is amazing.The food was also really good.
Big shout-out to Achini sewwandi from the staff. She was so friendly , helpful and always made sure I was comfortable . She honestly made my stay extra special.
Would definitely recommend this hotel if you're visiting Kandy

Name: OZO Hotel Kandy
User_Review: Radisson Hotel is the happiest hotel I have ever visited in my life. The staff is very nice and welcomed us with a beautiful smile. Even though they were tired, they never neglected our needs. I would especially like to mention Achini, Nipuni and Mahoda who treated us very lovingly. Thank you all for creating unforgettable memories for us ♥️

Name: OZO Hotel Kandy
User_Review:
not far from central of town, 10 mins walk along the lake front.

Simply awesome staff. Room is cleaned with sufficient supply of tea/coffee making facilities & toiletries too.

Will definitely stay here again for my next visit.

Thank you Kandy Radisson Hotel.

Regards
Tin

Name: The Golden Crown Hotel
User_Review:
The Golden Crown Hotel in Kandy is a beautiful and luxurious place with stunning views all around. The rooms are spacious and comfortable, and the gym is really good — well-equipped and clean, perfect for a workout while enjoying the view. The food is also delicious with a great variety to choose from.

However, the lift could be maintained better as it’s often not very clean, and the reception staff could be a bit more friendly and welcoming. Apart from that, everything else about the hotel is wonderful — the atmosphere, design, and facilities make it a great stay overall.

Name: The Golden Crown Hotel
User_Review: Everything is fantastic. Extremely clean, the staff are fantastic. The food is exceptional, we really enjoyed it. The pool is amazing. Everything you need is in the hotel. The driver was fantastic and very helpful, we enjoyed every moment. A truly wonderful experience!

Name: The Golden Crown Hotel
User_Review:
We had a great stay at The Golden Crown Hotel in Kandy. The property is elegant, well-maintained, and perfectly located for exploring the city while still offering a peaceful, relaxing atmosphere.

The rooms were clean and very comfortable, with beautiful views and all the amenities we needed. We especially enjoyed the buffet restaurant, which offered an excellent variety of both local and international dishes.

The staff were attentive and courteous, always ready to assist with a smile, their warmth made our stay even more enjoyable.

The hotel also features a great game room with a variety of activities, which was a fun bonus and perfect for unwinding after a day of exploring.

We would happily return and highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Kandy.

Name: The Golden Crown Hotel
User_Review:
We had a wonderful stay at the Golden Crown Hotel! From the moment I arrived, the staff were warm, welcoming, & always ready to help with a smile. The rooms are clean, spacious, & very comfortable, with everything you’d need for a relaxing stay.

The location is perfect, close to major attractions yet quiet enough to unwind after a busy day. We also really enjoyed the breakfast & dinner, which had a great variety & tasted fresh everyday.

Overall, Golden Crown Hotel exceeded our expectations. Highly recommend it for anyone looking for comfort, great service, & good value!

Name: The Golden Crown Hotel
User_Review:
We came to The Golden Crown for my birthday and we couldn’t believe the service.

The hotel is beautiful, but the staff make the experience. Everyone is unbelievably friendly and helpful, nothing is too much bother.

The breakfast buffet and the dinner buffet had so many delicious options it’s hard to choose, for some reason the in room dining food wasn’t as good, but honestly the buffets were absolutely incredible.

Rooms were beautiful, we had a balcony and the Mountain View was just amazing, the staff made such a huge effort to make our stay a great experience and I’d highly recommend anyone to stay here and wouldn’t go anywhere else if we returned to Kandy.

There’s a big pool with a stunning view, you can play pool or table tennis in another room, plus I had a massage which was incredible!

Overall: 10/10 hotel - Incredibly friendly and welcoming staff (honestly, so amazing) delicious buffets, beautiful hotel and rooms!

Name: Mountbatten Bungalow Kandy
User_Review: My husband and I stayed at the bungalow for 2 nights over the long weekend. We had an amazing time, everything was perfect. The staff were so friendly and special thanks to Chula who made us feel so comfortable. The  gardens were well maintained and the food was really good. The chef always checked in on us on our food preferences which was really nice. We also got a massage from the spa and the masseur was well trained. The view was great especially near the pool, however the only downside was the water in the pool was really cold. But we would love to visit the bungalow again.

Name: Mountbatten Bungalow Kandy
User_Review: We had the most wonderful stay in one of the garden chalets at the hotel. The staff were absolutely incredible, so attentive and friendly to our little toddler. The spa treatment / massage was great. We also went on the hotel-organised nature walk with an amazing guide. Saw lots of lovely birds and monkeys! The breakfast was good but the dinner food was a little lacking in flavour sometimes. Stunning views over the valley.

Name: Mountbatten Bungalow Kandy
User_Review:
We stayed in one of their rooms in the main building and not in a bungalow. The hotel is lovely, the room was cozy and spacious, shower was hot and the gardens/pool very nice. Staff are very helpful and polite. The only downside was the food. Maybe because we are in low season and the chef was not there but dinner was disappointing. Breakfast was ok but nothing extraordinary.
The hotel is located 30 min away from Kandy town (google says 20 but with the usual traffic if more than that).

Name: Mountbatten Bungalow Kandy
User_Review: We stayed one night at this quiet corner of Kandy. Exceptionally attentive staff. Stunning garden and pool. Huge bathroom with shower and bath. Generous dinner and breakfast. Home made curry powder. Not walkable to town but that is exactly why we chose it - quiet and serene.

Name: Mountbatten Bungalow Kandy
User_Review:
Mountbatten Bungalow located in a very quit area in Kandy.  The expeeince is amazing and unforgettable. Beautifully structured and styled  Building and rooms. swimming pool located is very scenic location to view themountains.

1 OF 6

Name: Jetwing St. Andrew’s Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: We spent three nights at Jetwing St.Andrew’s, Nuwara Eliya for our honeymoon and it was unforgettable. I actually forgot to request the honeymoon setup in advance, but the team didn’t miss a beat—they arranged everything the very next day, complete with a lovely chocolate cake, beautiful Azaleas flowers deco. The property’s colonial charm, cozy fireplaces, and mist-kissed gardens made the whole stay feel romantic and peaceful. Our room was spotless, warm, and quiet. Dining was excellent from breakfast to dinner. Every interaction felt genuine and caring—from check-in to checkout, nothing was too much trouble. Special thanks to Gawri and Kavish for helping with everything and making sure our late request turned into a perfect memory. Their kindness and efficiency truly stood out.

Name: Jetwing St. Andrew’s Nuwara Eliya
User_Review:
We stayed at Jetwing St. Andrew’s from the 6th to the 7th with my husband and our three kids, and it was a truly memorable experience.

From the moment we arrived, we were impressed by how beautifully the hotel has preserved its colonial charm. it feels like stepping back in time, yet with all the modern comforts you’d expect. The atmosphere is peaceful and elegant, perfect for a family getaway in Nuwara Eliya.

The food was delicious, with a variety of choices that our kids also enjoyed, a big plus for families. The staff were incredibly friendly and attentive throughout our stay. A special thanks to Vivekeshan, Ananda, Shane and the entire F&B crew for their warm hospitality and excellent service. We also want to appreciate Gowry from the front office for her care and kindness.

Overall, Jetwing St. Andrew’s is not just a hotel; it’s an experience filled with history, warmth, and genuine Sri Lankan hospitality. We’ll definitely return on our next visit to Nuwara Eliya.

Name: Jetwing St. Andrew’s Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: Jetwing St. Andrew’s made our honeymoon unforgettable. My husband chose this charming hotel, and I’m so grateful he did. From the moment we arrived, every team member was warm, kind, humble, and incredibly supportive. We especially enjoyed our conversations with Gawri and Kaveesh—two wonderful souls who made us feel truly cared for. The atmosphere is serene, the hospitality is top-notch, and every detail is thoughtfully handled. If you’re looking for a beautiful, relaxing escape in Nuwara Eliya with exceptional service, I highly recommend Jetwing St. Andrew’s. Thank you for a perfect stay!

Name: Jetwing St. Andrew’s Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: This is a truly fabulous hotel.  The staff are fantastic.  The food is incredible.  It is a historic building and there are some functional issues with that.  The walls are a bit thin.  We were in a family attic room and the stairs were very narrow - I wouldn't have wanted to be in it with young children.  It is actually cold, and was cold in the dining room - but just bring sweaters to wrap up warm.  They celebrated my husband and my birthday (same day) very well and made us feel like honoured guests.  It is a piece of history and I believe it is worth it despite some of the slight drawbacks.

Name: Jetwing St. Andrew’s Nuwara Eliya
User_Review:
We absolutely loved our stay at Jetwing St. Andrew’s. The hotel itself is incredibly charming, with a warm and welcoming atmosphere from the moment you arrive. The staff were nothing short of amazing, friendly, attentive, and always ready to help with a smile.

The architecture and history of the building make the experience even more special. You can really feel the character of its past in every corner, from the elegant woodwork to the colonial details that tell the story of another era.

A perfect blend of old-world charm and exceptional hospitality. Highly recommended!

Name: Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: We stayed at the Grand for night while on a 2 week trip to Sri Lanka for our honeymoon. On arrival we were greeted with a cup of tea and very friendly staff! Our room was fantastic with a lovely cake waiting for us to celebrate! The cocktail bar was sophiscated yet cozy and the food throughout was amazing! Ajanthra, Nathu and  Bilal were especially accommodating and my husband loved talking about the cricket. The gardens are also a must see. We wish we had stayed another night!

Name: Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: We did not stay at the hotel, but we visited for the afternoon high tea, and it was a wonderful experience. The hotel has a classic colonial charm and an elegant setting that makes the atmosphere very special. The high tea itself was well presented, with a nice selection of teas, savory bites, and sweet treats that were fresh and delicious. The service was attentive and professional, and enjoying it in the cool Nuwara Eliya climate with the beautiful garden surroundings made the visit even more memorable. It was a truly enjoyable experience, and I would highly recommend stopping by for high tea when in Nuwara Eliya

Name: Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya
User_Review:
Great place to stay. Beautiful manicured gardens. Good food, good service. Rooms are a tad small, though the bathrooms of a good size. Lack of slip-resistant mat in the tub was the only negative. It is a heritage building from the mid nineteenth century which has been converted into a luxury property.
Large range of cuisine options, great breakfast.

Name: Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya
User_Review:
We had the pleasure of dining at the Thai restaurant at the Grand Hotel, Nuwara Eliya, and it was an experience to remember. From the moment we walked in, the ambience was warm and inviting, setting the perfect tone for the evening. The service was attentive and truly first-class, with every detail taken care of effortlessly.

The food was exceptional! Served promptly, beautifully presented, and full of authentic flavor. The cocktails were simply fabulous, and the desserts were absolutely divine, the kind you would return for again and again. Even the washrooms were spotless and well-maintained, reflecting the hotel’s high standards.

Having visited the Grand Hotel for many years, it continues to remain one of our all-time favourite places. With its consistency in quality and hospitality, it’s a spot we will always look forward to revisiting whenever we are in Nuwara Eliya.

Name: Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya
User_Review:
We celebrated our engagement at the Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya, and it was truly magical. The colonial charm of the hotel, combined with its beautiful gardens and scenic views, created the perfect backdrop for such a special moment.

The staff were incredibly attentive and made everything seamless,from arrangements to service, they went above and beyond to ensure our day was memorable. The ambiance, the food, and the attention to detail made it an unforgettable experience.

I couldn’t have imagined a more perfect place to celebrate such an important milestone in our lives. Highly recommended for anyone looking for elegance and a personal touch in Nuwara Eliya.

Name: Araliya Green Hills
User_Review:
We visited this hotel for their dinner buffet to celebrate my husband’s birthday, and it was an absolutely wonderful experience! The food and buffet spread were amazing — every dish was delicious and well presented.

The service was outstanding, especially from the Restaurant and Bar Manager, Mr. Benedic Netto, who went above and beyond to make our evening special. He personally took care of everything and even arranged a lovely cake-cutting moment with live piano music.

It was truly a pleasure choosing this place for our special day — they made it even more memorable with their warm hospitality and attention to detail. Highly recommended! Definitely will visit them for a stay.

Name: Araliya Green Hills
User_Review: If I could, I’d give them 4.5 stars. The hotel is located a little bit away from the main highway with a cluster of other hotels and B&Bs. It is also very walkable to the town center as well as to Lake Gregory. The rooms were sufficiently adorned and though I was disappointed that they did not have air conditioning, they provided portal fans that were sufficient given the mild weather in the area (year round). They even have an indoor pool and a small, but functional gym. The staff really go above and beyond to ensure that your stay is enjoyable and pleasant. Overall a great place to stay for groups, families and couples.

Name: Araliya Green Hills
User_Review:
We just checked out today after a 2-night stay at this hotel. While the property is dated, it is reasonably clean.

Unfortunately, we encountered several issues related to organization and guest management. For example, the extra bed we had booked was not in the room upon arrival, and we had to request it twice before it was finally provided. Additionally, our room was not cleaned daily during our stay.

However, the most disappointing aspect of our experience was the treatment received in the restaurant. We couldn't help but notice a clear difference in how we were treated compared to local guests or returning customers.
On our first night at dinner, we were seated in a back section of the restaurant and left unattended for over 15 minutes. It was only after we approached the staff that a table was arranged for us. On the second evening, we were placed at a table near the kitchen and service passage. After requesting a change, we were reluctantly moved to a different table — the staff's attitude during this interaction felt unwelcoming.
That evening, we were served a soda with a visibly rusted bottle cap. When we pointed it out, the issue was reported to the restaurant manager, who showed no concern whatsoever and simply turned away.
Please note that we were traveling with two small children, which made the lack of attention and hospitality even more disappointing.
Even at breakfast, we observed that some guests were served coffee or hot tea at the table, and even had buffet items brought to them — something that was never offered to us.

Overall, there seemed to be a significant inconsistency in how guests were treated, depending on who they were. From a 4-star hotel, this is simply unacceptable.

I currently work in the tourism industry, and I have never experienced such a lack of professionalism in guest service.

On a positive note, the spa deserves special mention — the therapist was exceptionally skilled and kind

Name: Araliya Green Hills
User_Review: Its apartment hotel. I booked 837 sq ft twin room apartment and hotel manager kasun said book another room for 8 and 11 yr old daughter. Sachin first came said 20 usd charge per day and then said book one more room for 2 children. Disaster service. And at checkout said pay again 40 usd, i showed him charge and the reception was like oh sorry. Its apartment hotel with great location. Not a 4 star. Nowhere near

Name: Araliya Green Hills
User_Review: “Thank you very much for the excellent service and delicious food. We truly enjoyed my stay and appreciate the warm hospitality from all the staff — especially Mr. Shashiranga for providing such tasty and well-prepared meals. Keep up the great work!”

Name: Heritance Tea Factory
User_Review:
We love this hotel and location so much!! This is our second time visiting. Such a unique hotel and location. So relaxing, peaceful and beautiful.
Highly recommend going on the 630am nature walk. Our guide was so knowledgeable and we learned and saw so much we would not have on our own. Very memorable walk with a tour guide we will never forget!!
Also recommend having some spa treatments. Great service at the spa from both of the ladies.
We also did the high tea on our last day. It’s very gourmet with a lovely presentation that was unforgettable. Kavikanthan did a great job as our waiter.
Sad to leave this special place.

Name: Heritance Tea Factory
User_Review: Beautiful property in the middle of tea gardens. The property has retained the old tea factory feel and blended with new amenities. The breakfast was delicious. Dosa was the best we’ve had so far in the last 6 days in this trip, probably the best I’ve had ever. Ganesh was our server for breakfast who was awesome and brought us extra hot tea and oats during the buffet. Overall a great stay

Name: Heritance Tea Factory
User_Review:
It is a wonderful place on the hills of Sri Lanka. The  hotel is very well located, with nice surroundings. The staff in very kind, especially Harsha, our favourite.
The rooms are very comfortable, and quiet. We spent excellent days here.

Name: Heritance Tea Factory
User_Review:
Hello,
This is by Sathsara & Dilshan. We visited Tea Factory on 01st Oct and stayed one night there. We have been 03 heritance hotels before actually tea factory was the best out of them. Clean rooms, friendly staff, delicious food and the environment around the location was attractive & amazing.  We must write about the friendly service given by Mr. Mohan Raj. Actually he was kind and giving his best to visitors. Each and everyone in the staff have smiley and pleasant looks. Thanks again for the superb service by the entire team. Ms. Jayanthi gave us a real tea plucking experience as well.She also a very humble person. Considering all these, I can Highly recommend the tea factory hotel without any doubt. 🤍🤍🤍

Name: Heritance Tea Factory
User_Review:
As we arrived Shanka & Nimamn greeted us with a genuinely friendly smile at the reception.

At the restaurant,
Yogaraj, Harsha, Gautham, Imantha, Kavi-Kanth, Wije, Samira, Dihan, Jaya Chandra, Namal, Nathan were extremely friendly, helpful, communicative & professional.  They all went out of their way to show what a good service they can offer.  A big thank you to them and they definitely deserve a pay rise.

Kishna and the new guy in the tea plucking area were also extremely sweet, friendly and professional. Krishna was very knowledgeable about what she taught us. Throughly enjoyed the tea plucking experience with her. We learnt alot.

Mohan Raj took us on a 2hr walk around the whole estate and he was very friendly, had very good communication skills and was confident about what he spoke. Throughly enjoyed the walk.

Jayaprakash and Nishantha who served us in the capacity of room service was also very obliging and helpful with a big smile.

Had a fabulous experience and your staff contributed to it in a big way and they were always in a happy mood and smiling

Kindest regards,

Amali Disanayaka
Priyanthi Benedict
Suhashini Fernando
Daniel Bwanika
Saroja Edmund

Name: Ceylon Tea Trails Bungalow
User_Review: Ceylon Tea Trails exceeded our expectations. From the location, ambiance, food, staff etc. My husband and I stayed in the Norwood Bungalow and it was absolutely stunning! The gardens were well manicured and the rooms/décor were well presented. It feels like you’ve been taken back in time. The whole experience is next to no other. The staff were one of the main highlights of our stay. The staff were very friendly, accommodating and made us feel comfortable. The food was amazing too!! Oh and the views were incredible!! Overall I would highly recommend Ceylon Tea Trails. Thank you Ceylon Tea Trails for a memorable and perfect escape.

Name: Ceylon Tea Trails Bungalow
User_Review: The most perfect relaxing retreat in the Hill Country. The service and food is exemplary, nothing is too much trouble. Approx 30min from Hatton TrainStation & close the Seaplane at Castlereigh reservoir. Walk around the tea plantations, swim in the pool & switch off in Tea Planters Bungalow. Room Fan instead of aircon but it is cooler in the Hills  and gives the rooms that authentic colonial feel.

Name: Ceylon Tea Trails Bungalow
User_Review: By far, the best hospitality experience we have had in our lives! From our room to the service to the food! Amazing!!

Name: Ceylon Tea Trails Bungalow
User_Review: Amazing place. Need to thank all the staff especially anoj who had an eye for all our needs.

Name: Ceylon Tea Trails Bungalow
User_Review: Wow - amazing experience in the Norwood Bungalow taking service and luxury to a very high level. Blown away by the hospitality, the quality of the food and the views over the tea plantations from the hot outdoor jacuzzi. Thanks to Singh and the team for two fantastic days

Name: Galway Heights Hotel
User_Review: Galway Gights Nuwaraeliya the first impression of the hotel is its quiet location and stately architecture. The inside of the hotel is very cosy, club room with activity and library. Food quality is very good with multi-cuisine offering. The dosa and sambar was very tasty and probably one of the best in the region. Highly recommended!!

Name: Galway Heights Hotel
User_Review:
Our experience at this luxurious hotel was out of this world. From a grand welcome to scrumptious arrat food, well prepared by qualified chefs.
The staff is very well trained and respectful.
The serene surroundings make for a relaxing vacation away from the city.
Thank you GALWAYand keep up the good work  Until we meet again, soon

Name: Galway Heights Hotel
User_Review:
We spent a few days at this beautiful hotel, and will recommend it to anyone looking for a peaceful, serene and relaxing experience away from the hustle and bustle of the town.
The food prepared on-site was delicious with well-trained and professional staff in attendance. Thank you Galway, hope to be back!

Name: Galway Heights Hotel
User_Review: What a wonderful place! The decoration is tasteful, the rooms are big, service is outstanding. A relaxing heaven only one km away from the Nuwara Eliya madnesses. Will definitely be back.

Name: Galway Heights Hotel
User_Review: We really enjoyed our stay at Galway heights. We were welcomed with a delicious cup of pumpkin soup, due to the cooler climate.  And seated in the relaxing bar area. The team were very warm to welcome us and Alex took us to walk in the tea fields before we visited the main town. The room was very spacious and very clean. The food was nice in the restaurant and the service was excellent. Because we were leaving early to travel onwards, the hotel team made us breakfast to go in pack up boxes and it was more than sufficient for a breakfast! The hotel has a billiards room, a smoking room (with Shisha) and a spacious bar area (Dubling bar) including live music and delicious cocktails. Highly recommend if you are traveling in the area.

Name: Serene Kings Villa Nuwara Eliya
User_Review:
Very good service! Nice food.
The only thing is the rooms are old, so when you walk in and sleep in the room, you can smell the old carpet...not very pleasent...

Apart from that, every thing is fantastic! Beautiful view and nice country side

Oh...there's nothing for you to shop nearby, so you are kind of trap in the villa.
So, it would be ideal if you just stay for the night.

Name: Serene Kings Villa Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: Our experience at Serene Kings Villa was extraordinary. The location, enterior, restorent and food were wonderful and unforgettable. Staff and service was unmatched. Worth exploring with family and friends.

Name: Serene Kings Villa Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: Great staff. Awsome food. Luxurios rooms with juccuzi. Beautiful garden. It has its own organic vegetable farm. U can pick your own veggies for your meal.

Name: Serene Kings Villa Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: Beautiful location, fantastic views and excellent personalized service by the staff. Special mention to Mr Shiva and Mr Rosary who went out of their way to make our stay so comfortable and enjoyable.

Name: Serene Kings Villa Nuwara Eliya
User_Review: Amazing location and really comfortable rooms. The views over the race course and a beautiful garden are a must see!

Name: Araliya Red Hotel
User_Review:
Recently, we visited Araliya Red during our trip to Sri Lanka. It was the largest hotel in Nuwara Eliya and exuded an absolutely stunning appearance.

Despite its impressive exterior, the hotel fell short in several key areas. For instance, after our check-in, they simply directed us to our room without any assistance or guidance, leaving us to carry our luggage ourselves. This was a significant oversight, especially considering the amenities we expected from a hotel of its caliber.

Furthermore, the noise levels at night were excessive, making it difficult to sleep soundly.

Another disappointment was the lack of dental kits, which were charged for. While this may not seem like a major issue, it was puzzling why they were not included in the room rate.

Lastly, we had to carry our luggage and bags ourselves during the checkout process, which was another inconvenience.

Despite these shortcomings, the hotel did have some positive aspects. The rooms were spacious and comfortable, and the restaurant offered delicious food that we thoroughly enjoyed. Additionally, the location of the hotel was convenient and well-placed.

In my opinion, with some improvements in service and amenities, Araliya Red has the potential to be a great hotel.

Name: Araliya Red Hotel
User_Review:
We stayed at the Araliya Red Hotel for one night in July 2025. This is a very large, grandiose property with an architectural style and interior design that feels somewhat Russian, Eastern European, or even influenced by Chinese or Korean aesthetics.

Pros:
•	Massive rooms: Spacious and well-furnished, though they still felt oddly empty and dark.
•	Excellent breakfast: An extensive buffet with a wide variety of options, live cooking stations, and attentive service. This was the highlight of our stay.
•	Indoor pool & bar service: The pool area looks impressive, with a separate kids’ pool and a bar.

Cons:
•	Atmosphere: Despite its size, the hotel felt quiet to the point of being lonely, and the dim hallways bordered on spooky.
•	Pool temperature: The indoor pool was not heated enough, which is disappointing in a cool-climate location like Nuwara Eliya.
•	Ethical concerns: I later learned the hotel is connected to the brother of a former Sri Lankan Prime Minister, raising concerns for me about nepotism, cronyism, and possible corruption.
•	Surroundings: The hotel overlooks areas of visible poverty, creating a stark contrast that felt uncomfortable.
•	Price vs. experience: We paid around AUD $150 for the night. While not unreasonable, the overall feel didn’t match my preferences or values.

Overall:
The Araliya Red offers a lavish setting with a standout breakfast, but for me, the emptiness, lack of warmth (both literal and figurative), and political associations outweighed the positives. I’d be more inclined to choose a smaller, more personal guesthouse next time.

Name: Araliya Red Hotel
User_Review:
My wife and I went to Araliya Red's dinner buffet for our anniversary and it was a great experience. Mr. Dasun handled our reservation in such a short notice and ensured everything was arranged perfectly.

From the moment we arrived, we were warmly welcomed, and the thoughtful arrangements made our special occasion feel even more memorable. The staff were attentive throughout, and the dinner service was exceptional. The buffet had an impressive spread with a wide variety of delicious dishes to enjoy.

A heartfelt thank you to the entire team for their outstanding service and for making our celebration such a memorable one.

Name: Araliya Red Hotel
User_Review:
I recently stayed at Araliya Red Hotel in Nuwara Eliya, and unfortunately, my experience was far from satisfactory. The main issue I encountered was that the rooms are not soundproof at all. From the moment I arrived, I could hear loud conversations, television noise, and even footsteps from neighboring rooms. It made it very difficult to relax or get a proper night’s sleep, which is something you definitely expect from a hotel of this standard.

Additionally, the lack of soundproofing makes the environment feel chaotic and stressful rather than calm and restful. Even the slightest noises from the hallway, staff, or other guests were clearly audible inside the room. This really impacted my overall stay because a hotel room should feel like a private sanctuary, not a shared space with no privacy.

While the location of the hotel is convenient and some of the staff were polite, the noise issue completely overshadowed any positives. I would not recommend staying here if you value peace, quiet, or a good night’s rest. There are other hotels in Nuwara Eliya that offer a more comfortable and relaxing experience.

Name: Araliya Red Hotel
User_Review:
Spacious clean rooms well stocked with required amenities.
Friendly and helpful staff. They take the effort to check out if anything is required.
The breakfast and dinner buffets had a great spread. Lots of vegetarian options available. The food was tasty and excellent.
The souvenir shop in the arcade is well stocked and reasonable.
Great view from the sky deck.
Will definitely return

Name: Heaven Seven Hotel
User_Review: The room was great! Super spacious, cosy and amazing views over the lake and hills around. Lovely balcony too.However, 1GB cap per person for WiFi is extremely awful for today’s standards.  The food was not plated very well. The staff were friendly and were attentive.

Name: Heaven Seven Hotel
User_Review: They got half of our group of 16 people sick dining and having breakfast at this hotel… Room soundproofing is non existant so you are going to hear everything that is going on both inside and outside the hotel as though you have a left a window opened… Disappointing experience that we would not recommend

Name: Heaven Seven Hotel
User_Review: We were a group of  friends travelling together and this hotel was booked through our travel agent. Though the hotel rooms were good and spacious the food was ok. The manager refused to provide a buffet dinner saying that the occupancy was less though there were quite a few guests staying there.This is inspite of the bookings which clearly said that both breakfast and dinner would be a buffet. This  amounts to plain cheating.. u charge us for a buffet and then just give us a set menu

Name: Heaven Seven Hotel
User_Review: The food was cold. The lock in our room was broken.  The room was super cold and there was no heating, late in the evening to early in the morning it was super noisy and echos, they didnt inform us about cleaning the room because apparently there was a card in the closet that you had to hang to your door, so they didnt clean the room in the 2 days we stayed there

Name: Heaven Seven Hotel
User_Review:
Glad we found heaven Seven . The maintenance, rooms , food specially da way they presented the food was a treat to da eyes, there was a small spread in the buffet but everything we had was tasty. We accidentally found this place and they even have Koko payment available.
Special thanks for Mr Farook and Mr priyadarshana and the entire staff who took good care of us during our stay and made us feel like home.
Definitely well come back next year too.
Highly recommend

Name: Little England Cottages
User_Review: The Little England Cottage is one of the most beautiful places I’ve ever visited in my life the location and setting are truly stunning. However, I was disappointed with the restaurant and bar experience. The staff seemed to lack proper knowledge about the products, and hygiene standards could be much better. Many drinks displayed on the shelves were not actually available, which was frustrating, and the bar menu rates were even higher than those in 5-star hotels. On top of that, orders took a very long time to prepare. If these areas are improved, this place has the potential to be absolutely incredible.

Name: Little England Cottages
User_Review:
Pedro Barn – Our Home Away from Home

At first, Pedro Barn didn’t sweep me off my feet — but after 5–6 stays (always in our balcony room), it’s now the only place we think of for a getaway. The comfort, the view, and the peaceful vibe make it feel like our own private escape. And yes, the restaurant downstairs deserves a standing ovation — their crab fried rice is so good it should have its own fan club. The staff are warm, attentive, and make sure every stay is effortless. Every visit here is a beautiful mix of relaxation, great food, and genuine hospitality — a place that truly grows on you.

Name: Little England Cottages
User_Review: I stayed at Pedro Barn at Little England Cottages and was extremely disappointed. For the price of $100–$150 per night, you’d expect basic comforts, but there’s no heater, no in-room bonfire, cheap interior, and zero English cottage charm apart from the name. The breakfast is limited with no buffet, and the facilities are nowhere near what they charge for. The only good thing about this place is its location, but that alone doesn’t justify the cost. Save your money and look for better options!

Name: Little England Cottages
User_Review: We weren’t allowed to dine inside as they said it was only for in-house guests — even though the entire restaurant was empty. It was raining and freezing outside, yet they didn’t make any exception. Strangely, once they saw our satisfactory order, they suddenly allowed us in. Very disappointing and unprofessional experience. Definitely not returning. 👎👎👎

Name: Little England Cottages
User_Review: Absolutely breathtaking location! The architecture takes you back to colonial-era England, blended beautifully with the misty hills of Nuwara Eliya. The cottages are cozy, clean, and offer a luxurious yet homely feel. Staff was incredibly helpful and friendly. Perfect for a romantic getaway or peaceful family vacation.

Name: Jetwing Lighthouse Galle
User_Review:
We recently stayed at the beautiful Jetwing Lighthouse in Galle with my wife and toddler, and it was nothing short of amazing. This was actually our second visit, and it only reminded us why we keep coming back!

We stayed at the Maharaja Suite with a private plunge pool, and it was perfect—spacious, comfortable, and with an absolutely stunning view. The attention to detail in the suite made our stay extra special.

A very special mention goes to Abishek, our butler, who took incredible care of us throughout our stay. From attending to every little detail to making sure our toddler was happy and comfortable, his service was warm, thoughtful, and truly world-class.

The food was delicious with plenty of variety, and the kids’ menu was really well thought out, which made dining stress-free with our little one. The wide variety of drinks available was also impressive. Every staff member we interacted with was friendly, pleasant, and genuinely welcoming, which added to the overall experience.

Overall, this has been another wonderful stay at Jetwing Lighthouse. With the level of comfort, service, and hospitality we experienced, we’ll definitely be visiting again. Highly recommended for families looking for luxury and relaxation in Galle!

Name: Jetwing Lighthouse Galle
User_Review:
This is undoubtedly one of the best places in Galle. From the moment we arrived until our departure, every experience was smooth and incredible.

We were warmly welcomed by the staff, who provided excellent service and hospitality. The views were breathtaking, and the facilities were well-maintained. Everything was perfect here.

The service and hospitality were top-notch, from the food to the room service. We even had a unique request to feed a hungry stray cat, and they happily obliged.

The property boasts two enormous swimming pools and a restaurant with a stunning sea view. A poolside bar and restaurant. A spa, gym, sun bathing are. The vast size of the property will not bore you.

We thoroughly enjoyed our stay and would definitely love to visit again soon.

Name: Jetwing Lighthouse Galle
User_Review: I had a truly wonderful experience at Jetwing Lighthouse in Galle. From the moment I arrived, the service was warm, attentive, and professional, making me feel completely at ease. The atmosphere is peaceful and relaxing, with a stunning beach view that adds to the charm of the stay. The food was excellent, offering both variety and great taste, perfectly complementing the overall experience. Whether it was enjoying the serene surroundings, the breathtaking ocean views, or the consistently good service, everything came together to create a memorable visit. A perfect place to unwind and enjoy the best of Galle.

Name: Jetwing Lighthouse Galle
User_Review:
A beautiful place designed by Geoffrey Bawa. His style was evident in the wide open spaces and the use of the land as it is. You can hear the sea from most places of the hotel.
There was only a small strip of a beach, with more of shells than sand! But its absence was not felt and that is amazing! The Sunset and even the sunrise can be ethereal, if you are fortunate to be there on a good day.

Rooms were spacious and had all the facilities one needs to just relax.
Food was great, super stuff for the mains - good variety, speed and taste, pastries and desserts were ok but not that great.

We did the Heritage tour with Jayan who was very good, knew his stuff and could get it across well.

Lucky Dissanayake's staircase was a treat to explore Sadly, some pieces were missing, especially the section with the archers - a picture was there in the gallery and that looked amazing. Hope they get it restored.

Staff was great - super efficient, polite and with the right amount of friendliness to make you feel welcome. The main restaurant staff needs a special mention, they were simply  was fantastic.

There was a cultural show on Saturday afternoon by a group of local artists. They showcased some traditional dances from Southern Sri Lanka.  It was great but would have been better if a short introduction was given to each of the items.

Thank you Jetwing Lighthouse, we had a lovely stay.

Name: Jetwing Lighthouse Galle
User_Review:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

An Architectural Masterpiece in Galle – Jetwing Lighthouse

Jetwing Lighthouse in Galle is one of the most stunning hotels I have visited. Designed by the world-renowned architect Geoffrey Bawa, it perfectly blends modern luxury with heritage and nature. The design is breathtaking, from the famous spiral staircase to the open spaces that capture the sound of the ocean.

The location is incredible – perched on a rocky headland, it gives panoramic views of the Indian Ocean while still being just minutes away from the historic Galle Fort. Watching the sunrise and sunset here is an unforgettable experience.

The food is exceptional. From fresh seafood to authentic Sri Lankan dishes and international cuisine, every meal is prepared with care and great flavor. The dining venues – especially the Cinnamon Room and Cardamom Café – make eating here a highlight of the stay.

Jetwing Lighthouse is not just a hotel, it’s an experience of Galle’s culture, history, and beauty. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a luxurious yet authentic escape in Sri Lanka’s south coast.

✅ Best for couples, families, and anyone who loves architecture, good food, and ocean views.

Name: Amari Galle Sri Lanka
User_Review:
It was a 2N/3D stay in radisson blu resort and it was a terrible experience. The so called sea view rooms were extremely bad, you can never watch the sea peacefully .. the balcony doors were so hard, we had to put all our strength to pull it.. & then when you open, the rooms get damp & floor becomes completely wet due to condensation. They could have easily avoided this by placing a carpet for the rooms. The bathrooms were also equally bad & cramped, dint feel like a 5 star property at all. There was a push door, then a slide door for such a small space which made it really uncomfortable in every way. Even the shower space , shower pipe, health faucet were setup in such a way that they wer not easy to use at all.

Service was okay. They did take some time to answer our requests.

Buffet breakfast was decent and swimming pool was the only best thing in the entire property.

The property was built to accommodate as many as rooms possible , hence it failed to serve the purpose of a resort. You could literally hear all the sounds of neighbouring rooms, pulling chairs, tables, tv sound.. it was so irritating and dint feel relaxing at all even in the night hours

Don't recommend this property at all.

Name: Amari Galle Sri Lanka
User_Review:
I stayed at Radisson Blu Resort Galle on 5th and 6th October 2025, and my experience was absolutely amazing! The hotel, service, and overall atmosphere were excellent.

What impressed me the most was their honesty and helpfulness — I accidentally left my backpack at the hotel while I was already halfway to Colombo on the highway. The hotel staff immediately called to inform me, and Chamesh went above and beyond to ensure my bag was safely returned to me.

Thank you for the great service and genuine care. Keep up the excellent work, Radisson Blu Resort

Name: Amari Galle Sri Lanka
User_Review:
Our stay at the hotel was truly memorable and exceeded our expectations in every way.
From the moment we arrived, the service was warm, welcoming, and attentive. One of the highlights of our stay was the rooftop bar – the views were absolutely stunning, especially during sunset. It was the perfect spot to unwind in the evenings with a great selection of drinks, good music, and a relaxing atmosphere. We spent several evenings there just enjoying the vibe.
We also really enjoyed the animation activities provided by the hotel. There was always something happening, which kept us entertained throughout our stay. The team managing the activities was enthusiastic and made sure guests of all ages felt included and had fun.
A very special mention must go to Thilini, who looked after us in the restaurant during breakfast. Every morning, she welcomed us with a warm smile, engaging conversation, and genuine care. She remembered our preferences and went out of her way to make sure we were comfortable and happy. Her kindness and professionalism truly stood out and added a personal touch to our experience.
Overall, this was one of the best hotel stays we’ve had. The combination of excellent service, beautiful facilities, great food, and the efforts of amazing staff like Thilini made it unforgettable. We’re already looking forward to our next visit!

Name: Amari Galle Sri Lanka
User_Review:
My friend and I stayed two nights at Radisson hotel!  We enjoyed our stay, staff were so friendly and attentive, welcomed us with refreshing drink.  Rooms were spacious and clean. We were upgraded and our room was on 7th floor with pool and sea view.
Food in the restaurant was delicious and was spoilt with choice of food.  Dil Shan looked after us, he was so helpful, he went out of his way to make coffee for my friend the way she likes it.  Hotel is so near to attractions .  Hotel has pool bar and roof top bar.

Name: Amari Galle Sri Lanka
User_Review: Absolutely stunning place to stay. I am writing this on our last day staying here and very sad to go. Location is amazing. The staff are absolutely incredible and always wanting to help. The grounds are maintained exceptionally well.. The room service is excellent. Used the laundry service too and clothes came back smelling lovely and clean. Lots of care taken.  The pool and cabanas are lobby- enjoyed hours here No

Name: Le Grand Galle Hotel
User_Review: Stayed at this amazing hotel and had an absolutely wonderful experience! The property is stunning, overlooking the Galle Fort and the vast Indian Ocean, with sprawling lawns that stretch right up to the water’s edge. The swimming pool is truly the highlight; it gives you an incredible feeling of being one with the ocean while you swim. The food was good, with a wide variety of flavours to enjoy, and the service was warm and attentive throughout. The location couldn’t be better, it’s close to everything Galle Fort, beach etc… yet peaceful and scenic. TukTuks are easily available at the hotel (book them using Uber). The hotel also has a lovely spa for relaxation and a cheerful happy hour every evening from 4:30 to 5:30 p.m. The best part, without a doubt, is the combination of the pool and the unbeatable oceanfront setting. A perfect place to unwind and soak in the beauty of Galle

Name: Le Grand Galle Hotel
User_Review:
The location of the hotel is beautiful. Fort and ocean views from every corner and beautifully appointed rooms. All staff highly attentive and helpful.

The breakfast is generous and well considered, with everything set out beautifully and lovely taste.

Our room was well maintained by Im Ishan, with bed towels designed into animals and a beautifully reset room. Thank you Im Ishan for your special touches.

Poolside service was prompt and the staff in the bar were kind and helpful, especially when the birds sometimes became an issue. They ensured towels were readily available and you had everything you needed.

Cocktail hour was an excellent addition to our afternoon relaxing with interesting drinks presented each day.

The only disappointment of the stay was a treatment in the spa. The room lacked the presence of a calming environment. No music or scents. The massage itself was poorly delivered, no modesty considerations and overall it lacked the skills of a trained therapist.

I would recommend highly a stay at Le Grande it is the perfect base for your stay in Galle with lots of luxuries. I just would not recommend getting a massage in the Spa.

Name: Le Grand Galle Hotel
User_Review:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We had such a wonderful stay at Le Grand Luxury Hotel! The rooms were exceptionally comfortable, the food was absolutely delicious, and the overall service was truly world-class. Every detail reflected great care and professionalism. A special mention to Madawantha from housekeeping for his excellent service and the thoughtful handwritten note that made our stay extra special. Thank you to the entire team for creating such a memorable experience!

Name: Le Grand Galle Hotel
User_Review:
We recently stayed at Le Grand Galle, and from the moment we arrived until the time we checked out, everything was simply exceptional. The location is truly unbeatable – perched by the ocean with stunning views of the historic Galle Fort and the endless Indian Ocean. Waking up to the sound of the waves and the sight of the sunrise from the balcony was an unforgettable experience.

The rooms were spacious, modern, and immaculately clean, offering both comfort and elegance. The bed was incredibly comfortable, and the bathroom was luxurious with a beautiful bathtub that looked out towards the sea. Every detail in the room was thoughtfully designed to make our stay pleasant and relaxing.

The staff were warm, attentive, and always ready to help with a smile. From check-in to dining to housekeeping, their service was consistently professional and friendly, which made us feel truly welcomed.

The food was another highlight – the breakfast spread had a wide variety of both local and international dishes, fresh fruits, and live cooking stations. We also tried dinner at the restaurant, and it was a delightful experience with fresh seafood and beautifully presented meals.

The infinity pool overlooking the ocean was a perfect spot to unwind. Sitting by the poolside in the evening with the cool breeze and a beautiful view was one of the most relaxing parts of our trip.

Safety and cleanliness standards were well maintained throughout the property, and we felt completely at ease during our stay.

Overall, Le Grand Galle is a perfect blend of luxury, comfort, and breathtaking views. Whether for a romantic getaway, a family vacation, or just a relaxing break, this hotel is an excellent choice. We left with wonderful memories and would definitely love to come back again.

Name: Le Grand Galle Hotel
User_Review: We spent one night at the hotel with our family and friends, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. The rooms were very clean and comfortable, which made our stay enjoyable. The pool was a highlight, especially for the kids, as it was both amazing and child-friendly. However, we did find that the food didn't quite meet our expectations, and the buffet lacked variety. With a few improvements, this place could truly be exceptional!

Name: Fort Bazaar Hotel Galle Fort
User_Review: What a treat to spend a few days at this characterful hotel in the heart of Galle Fort. The old world charm of Fort Bazaar and the beautiful people of Sri Lanka made us return to this stunning location yet again. The staff is warm and hospitable and Avi and his team went out of their way to make sure we had everything we needed. The rooms were clean and spacious and the stylish decor impressed us the moment we stepped inside. The poolside experience and the breakfast and happy hour extras were faultless! Loved everything!

Name: Fort Bazaar Hotel Galle Fort
User_Review: Fort Bazaar is such a great spot in the heart of Galle Fort — stylish, comfy, and full of charm. The rooms are beautiful, the food is delicious, and the staff are super friendly. Perfect place to chill and explore the area!

Name: Fort Bazaar Hotel Galle Fort
User_Review:
A beautiful experience at Fort Bazaar.
I arrived at 5am and was greeted by two welcoming night staff. The staff at the hotel are wonderful- friendly and knowledgable.
The hotel is incredibly charming and very well maintained. Restaurant and bar are a great place to have breakfast, and relax after a long walk around Galle. The spa treatments are also a perfect way to unwind- my deep tissue massage was best start to a restful stay at the Fort Bazaar.

Name: Fort Bazaar Hotel Galle Fort
User_Review:
Dropped in at Fort Bazaar for a light breakfast and much-needed coffee, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised! Being from Colombo, I honestly didn’t expect the coffee in Galle to turn out this good — but it was absolutely perfect. The food was just above perfect too, beautifully prepared and full of flavor.

The place itself looks elegant, with a lovely blend of Galle Fort antiques and modern furniture — such a charming vibe! Unfortunately, due to our tight morning schedule, I couldn’t explore the interiors or take more pictures, but it’s definitely worth a proper visit next time.

A special thanks to Amjaad, the manager, who spoke to us, suggested the best dishes, and personally ensured my coffee came out exactly how I wanted. Exceptional service, amazing food, and a beautiful setting — highly recommended to anyone visiting Galle Fort! 🍻🍻🍻

Name: Fort Bazaar Hotel Galle Fort
User_Review:
An Unforgettable Stay at Fort Bazaar Galle

I had the pleasure of staying at Fort Bazaar Galle, and it was truly an exceptional experience from start to finish. The moment I arrived, I was welcomed with warm hospitality and a beautifully designed space that perfectly blends colonial charm with modern comfort.

The rooms were impeccably maintained, offering a perfect balance of luxury and relaxation. Every detail, from the cozy bedding to the thoughtfully curated amenities, reflected the hotel’s dedication to excellence. The staff went above and beyond to ensure my stay was memorable, providing attentive yet unobtrusive service throughout.

One of the highlights of my visit was the delightful surprise from the team – a beautifully presented cake that added a personal touch to my stay. This thoughtful gesture truly showcased their commitment to creating unforgettable moments for their guests.

The dining experience at the hotel was also top-notch. The food was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared, making every meal an absolute delight. The central location within the Galle Fort made it easy to explore the historic surroundings, yet the hotel itself felt like a peaceful retreat.

If you’re looking for a luxurious yet welcoming place to stay in Galle, Fort Bazaar is an excellent choice. I highly recommend it and can’t wait to return!

Name: Tamarind Hill Galle
User_Review: It is an extraordinarily beautiful heritage hotel that offers extremely attentive butler service. During our stay, only two or three rooms in the entire hotel were occupied. We had booked the full-board package, and the hotel staff could customize your dining times according to your requests—you could dine at any time you wished. The food was particularly delicious with a wide range of menu options; every meal included a complete course of appetizer, soup, main course, and dessert. On the last day, as we had to get up early to catch a train, the hotel prepared breakfast for us as early as 6 o'clock. All staff members were always smiling, providing highly personalized and private services. This hotel is worth visiting for everyone and is truly value for money. The young waiter at the restaurant was extremely thoughtful. He pulled out chairs for us and got everything ready every time, and he was constantly attentive to any of our needs. When we mentioned that we wanted to have breakfast outside, he told us that there were too many mosquitoes at the moment and asked if we would like to do so for dinner instead. By evening, he had prepared everything perfectly: he set up a table in the courtyard, got lamps ready, and even arranged potted plants. It was truly wonderful.

Name: Tamarind Hill Galle
User_Review:
A Timeless Haven in Galle – Tamarind Hill

Tamarind Hill is a place I know I will keep returning to every time I am in Sri Lanka. This is a promise. Nestled on the edge of a quiet village in Galle, this two-hundred-year-old Dutch bungalow feels like a living poem in stone and silence. Steeped in history and wrapped in old-world charm, the property feels ethereal, suspended in time. Serene, secluded, and impossibly beautiful, it is the kind of place where you could lose yourself for weeks, well, I could for months.

Keshav and his team redefine hospitality. Their warmth is genuine, their attention to detail remarkable, the kind that makes you feel less like a guest and more like an old friend returning home. Every single member of that team deserves a raise.

I was travelling solo through Sri Lanka when I booked Tamarind Hill. Upon arrival, I discovered that I was the only guest, a rare lull for a property otherwise known for its exquisite food, as they were mending a gas leak that week. Now most solo female travellers might hesitate, and understandably so, but something about Keshav and his team instantly put me at ease. My instincts said I was safe, and they were right. For three quiet days, before the weekend brought a full house, I was the lone guest in this grand old bungalow, yet never once did I feel alone.

As a woman, you learn to trust your instincts about people and energies, and I am grateful that my intuition led me here. The kindness and integrity of the team at Tamarind Hill turned what could have been a daunting experience into one of the most peaceful and grounding stays of my travels. And I am full of gratitude that the team at Tamarin Hill was an eminent part of my Lanka journey.

The colonial style property in itself is a masterpiece. The suites upstairs, the rooms downstairs, the open courtyard, each space whispering stories of another era. The sounds of birds and a stillness that invites words to flow. A place that nourishes both solitude and imagination. And from what I hear your tummy and your soul.

I shall return for the food, for the timeless charm, and for the feeling of being so deeply cared for. I am, certainly, not done.

My apologies for the delayed review, Keshav and team. Perhaps it is only fitting, because Tamarind Hill is a place that lingers.

Name: Tamarind Hill Galle
User_Review: I had a wonderful stay at Tamarind Hill Hotel. The property offers a calm, colonial charm with spacious rooms tastefully decorated to reflect traditional Sri Lankan identity. Galle Fort is less than 5 km away, making it incredibly easy to explore the historic fort by a short tuk-tuk or car ride. The serene surroundings and attentive staff made my visit both relaxing and memorable. I’d happily recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Galle.

Name: Tamarind Hill Galle
User_Review: A lovely place to stay, where the renowned Sri Lankan hospitality is perfectly maintained. The manager is professional, and the team is wonderful. We had a fantastic time, and the food was excellent.

Name: Tamarind Hill Galle
User_Review:
Delightful, this is a charming property and has a heritage legacy to it. It has been wonderfully converted to a boutique hotel.

The rooms are ultra spacious and have a private lawn.

The space planning of rooms is such that there is a central lawn cum courtyard.

The pool is very well planned in a linear manner and is some levels down, it has a wonderful tropical vibe to it.

The staff was very nice and accommodating to us esp with our requirement of veg food. They really ensured we have the best of food with multiple veg options.

The manager in charge was extremely courteous and hospitable.

Great hospitality.

Name: The Fortress Resort & Spa Koggala
User_Review:
My stay at The Fortress Resort & Spa in Galle was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with genuine warmth and professionalism. The setting is breathtaking – a perfect blend of modern luxury and traditional Sri Lankan design, set right on the oceanfront.
Our room was spacious, beautifully designed, and offered stunning views of the sea. The attention to detail throughout the resort, from the decor to the landscaping, really adds to the peaceful and luxurious atmosphere.
The food was another highlight – every meal was delicious, with a great mix of local and international options. The staff were always attentive and kind, making sure we were comfortable and well taken care of throughout our stay.
Whether you're looking to relax by the pool, enjoy a spa treatment, or explore Galle Fort nearby, this resort is the perfect base. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a serene, high-end experience on the southern coast of Sri Lanka.

Name: The Fortress Resort & Spa Koggala
User_Review:
We celebrated our son's 1st B'day here at The Fortress. The hotel is amazing, the rooms spacious and the service is very warm and inviting.

One major criticism, which wasn't mentioned anywhere is that they don't have elevators. So, elderly people accompanying may have difficulty walking up stairs. Our parents had some difficulty and we had to rearrange / upgrade rooms last minute for extra price!

The staff is very friendly, the look and feel of the property is very pristine and we had a wonderful time there!

Name: The Fortress Resort & Spa Koggala
User_Review:
Sri Lanka never ceases to amaze — it truly leaves no stone unturned in surprising you. The Fortress Resort and Spa is among the best places I’ve ever stayed, from its stunning view from the ocean rooms to the exceptional hospitality throughout my visit.

When dining at the restaurant, be sure to ask for Chaminda and Tharushi — they go above and beyond to make your experience pleasant and memorable. Their genuine warmth, attentiveness, and passion for guest service truly stand out.

Thank you, The Fortress Resort and Spa, for making our stay so wonderful. I’m already looking forward to returning soon!

Name: The Fortress Resort & Spa Koggala
User_Review:
My stay at The Fortress Resort & Spa was amazing! The rooms were spotless, spacious, and beautifully designed.
The staff were incredibly kind and attentive, making me feel truly cared for.
The views were breathtaking especially at sunset & sunrise.
Everything about the place radiates comfort, cleanliness, and genuine hospitality. Highly recommend!

Name: The Fortress Resort & Spa Koggala
User_Review: This would have to be one of the nicest hotels I have stayed at, not just in Sri Lanka, but full stop. Beautiful architecture, facilities, and surroundings. The staff, from the front reception, to lifeguard at the pool, to the team at the restaurant were exceptional. So…why only 4 stars? When we collected our two vehicles to head back home, which were valet parked, they were absolutely covered with bird poo(Or maybe bat poo?…not sure, not an expert). The staff that would have driven the cars to the entry would have noticed this easily, yet said nothing. We had to pull after about a kilometre and wash the cars as it was so bad, and then again a more thorough wash once we got to Colombo. It was really disappointing that after the whole team and hotel just ‘Wowed’ us for two days, it was undone by letting this happen and leave a sour taste in our mouths as the last experience from the hotel. Leave the poo incident aside, Amazing hotel! Would love to come back for a week and just relax watching the waves. Next time I will Uber. Lol

Name: Cantaloupe Aqua Unawatuna
User_Review:
We were visiting down south and wanted to find a beautiful, clean place to relax. While searching on Agoda, we came across The Levels Unawatuna — it looked stunning in the photos and was close to Jungle Beach, so we decided to book a suite.

However, when we arrived, we were really disappointed. The hotel looked nothing like the pictures — it seemed poorly maintained and almost abandoned. When we checked into our suite, the room was unclean, and we had to ask several staff members to clean it. The furniture was old and worn out (understandable to some extent since it’s by the sea, but still not acceptable for the price we paid), and the washroom had visible stains.

After checking in, we went out to visit Galle Fort and returned around 9.30 p.m. to find that the AC wasn’t working. There was no one to contact except the security guard, who tried to get help. They couldn’t fix it, so around 11 p.m., we were moved to a smaller deluxe room, which was definitely not equivalent to the suite we had paid for.

The next morning, while at the dining area, the manager was there but didn’t even bother to acknowledge or apologize for what happened. When we checked out, the receptionist also didn’t mention anything, so we had to ask to speak to the manager ourselves. When she finally came, she was quite arrogant and dismissive, saying she couldn’t do anything since we had booked through Agoda.

Overall, it was a terrible experience and a complete waste of money. The hotel looks nothing like what’s shown online, and the management has no sense of responsibility or customer care. Would not recommend this place to anyone.

Name: Cantaloupe Aqua Unawatuna
User_Review:
A Truly Disappointing Stay: Mismanagement and Misleading Conduct

Our experience at this hotel was ruined by an unprofessional and dishonest manager who showed no understanding of basic hospitality or accountability.

We had booked two suites, but upon arrival and going to the room, we discovered that one suite’s AC was not working. Instead of offering an apology or proper solution, we were downgraded to a smaller “superior” room without any discussion.
